step_rationale: Cardiac injury biomarker; dysregulated in AMI and ischemia-reperfusion; involved in arrhythmia and platelet activation.
mechanism: miR-1 is released from ischemic myocardium and dysregulated in MI; elevated levels indicate cardiomyocyte injury and arrhythmia risk.

references.title: Human Heart Anoxia and Reperfusion Tissue (HEART) Model for the Rapid Study of Exosome Bound miRNA Expression As Biomarkers for Myocardial Infarction.
Over-expression of microRNA-1 causes arrhythmia by disturbing intracellular trafficking system.
Platelet microRNA for predicting acute myocardial infarction.
Circulating Long Noncoding RNA UCA1 as a Novel Biomarker of Acute Myocardial Infarction.
Circulating microRNA: a novel potential biomarker for early diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in humans.
Circulating microRNA-1 as a potential novel biomarker for acute myocardial infarction.
